title: Plural pronouns and colloquial ý → ej
summary: Use plural possessives and demonstratives, then recognize a common colloquial Bohemian pronunciation.
objectives: Choose plural possessive pronouns by gender and animacy
Use plural demonstratives ti, ty, and ta
Distinguish identifying To jsou from demonstratives before plural nouns
Recognize colloquial ej where standard Czech has ý

sections.heading: Plural possessive pronouns
sections.body: For masculine animate plural nouns use moji, tvoji, naši, and vaši. Masculine inanimate, feminine, and neuter plurals use moje, tvoje, naše, and vaše. Jeho, její, and jejich remain unchanged.

sections.heading: Plural demonstratives
sections.body: Use ti with masculine animate plural nouns, ty with masculine inanimate and feminine plural nouns, and ta with neuter plural nouns.

sections.heading: To jsou remains unchanging
sections.body: When identifying objects, use To jsou regardless of gender. Demonstratives agree only when they directly modify a noun.
examples: Ty kočky jsou tlusté. — Those cats are fat.
To jsou tlusté kočky. — Those are fat cats.

sections.heading: Colloquial ý becomes ej
sections.body: In colloquial speech across Bohemia and parts of Moravia, standard long ý is frequently pronounced ej. Learners should recognize this pattern but continue producing standard forms initially.

vocabulary.note: Masculine inanimate, feminine, or neuter plural.

resources.title: Possessive pronouns — plural
resources.note: Plural forms by gender and animacy.

resources.title: Demonstratives — plural
resources.note: Ti, ty, ta and unchanging To jsou.

resources.title: Colloquial pronunciation ý → ej
resources.note: A widespread pronunciation pattern to recognize passively.

exercises.explanation: Kamarádi are masculine animate plural, so use moji.
Okna are neuter plural, so the modifying demonstrative is ta.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence means Those are dirty windows?
exercises.explanation: Plural identification uses unchanging To jsou.

exercises.prompt: Type the standard Czech form corresponding to colloquial velkej.
exercises.explanation: Colloquial ej often corresponds to standard ý: velkej → velký.
